summary: Windows persistence + obfuscated payload eval + Microsoft-component masquerade on require
details: On require/launch, launcher.js (the package main) installs HKCU Run-key persistence under the name 'OneDriveUpdate' that points wscript.exe at a shipped 'OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs' which silently spawns node.exe on launcher.js with a hidden window. launcher.js then sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' (a real Windows system process), suppresses all uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers, and eval()s an 88KB obfuscator.io-style bundle (server.obf.js, 440-entry rotated string array, switch-case dispatcher) so the actual server behavior is hidden from inspection. A 3-second poll writes ad-hoc.ps1 files into the package directory and spawns powershell.exe with '-NoProfile -EP Bypass' to enumerate Chrome window titles for 'YouTube' and trigger conditional payloads when detected. The 'sens update' CLI runs 'taskkill /F /IM node.exe' and 'taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe' against every Node and PowerShell process on the host, then re-pulls 'npm i -g sensivity@latest' from a mutable tag, giving the author a rolling-update channel. Microsoft-component masquerade (Run-key value name, VBS filename, process.title) plus reboot persistence plus eval-of-obfuscated-blob plus environment-gated PowerShell execution is unambiguous malware tradecraft, not the advertised 'Mobile Control Panel' functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
